steps.title: Determine whether the appositive is restrictive or non-restrictive
steps.content:
The phrase "hexafluorobenzene (C6F6) and benzene (C6H6)" identifies which specific liquid compounds have those molar masses. Because it answers "which ones?" and is essential to the sentence's meaning, it is a restrictive (essential) appositive. Restrictive appositives take no commas.
  • Choice A — compounds, hexafluorobenzene (C6F6) and benzene (C6H6),: commas on both sides treat the names as non-essential, but they are essential identifiers. ✗
  • Choice B — compounds hexafluorobenzene (C6F6) and benzene (C6H6),: no opening comma but a trailing comma creates a mismatched, broken structure. ✗
  • Choice C — compounds, hexafluorobenzene (C6F6) and benzene (C6H6): an opening comma without a closing comma — still incorrectly sets off the essential appositive as non-restrictive. ✗
  • Choice D — compounds hexafluorobenzene (C6F6) and benzene (C6H6): no commas at all — correctly treats the names as a restrictive appositive essential to identifying which compounds. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Because the compound names are essential identifying information, no commas should appear around them. That is the correct answer.
